description: The Jheri Curls were a Dominican gang active in Washington Heights, Manhattan, New York City in the early 1990s. Named after the Jheri curl hairstyle, they ran a major cocaine trafficking operation based out of an apartment complex on W. 157th St. and Riverside Drive. Led by Rafael Martinez and his brothers, the gang was known for extreme violence, including the murder of a resident who cooperated with police. The gang was dismantled in October 1991 after undercover police operations led to arrests and lengthy prison sentences.

country_links.evidence_quote: The Jheri Curls were a Dominican gang which was active in the Washington Heights, Manhattan neighborhood of New York City in the early 1990s... the gang ran a major cocaine trafficking operation in upper Manhattan which was ultimately based out of an apartment complex on W. 157th St. and Riverside Drive.
country_links.context: The Jheri Curls ran a major cocaine trafficking operation in upper Manhattan, New York City, and were based there.
